title: betonica nivea subsp. nivea
intro: Betonica nivea subsp. nivea, also known as White Mountain Betony, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of iridoids and phenolic compounds. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including headaches, migraines, and insomnia, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. White Mountain Betony can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
